functions.name: concernbert.latex.dataframe_to_latex
documentation:
Converts a pandas DataFrame into a LaTeX table format following ACM guidelines.

Parameters:
- df: pandas DataFrame containing the table data.
- caption: The caption to be used for the table.
- label: The LaTeX label to reference the table.
- col_format: A string representing the column formatting (e.g., 'lrrrr' for left-aligned and right-aligned columns).
- wide_table: If True, uses the `table*` environment for wide tables, otherwise uses `table`.
- decimal_places: Optional; if provided, truncates numerical values to the specified number of decimal places.

Returns:
- A string containing the LaTeX table.
Written by ChatGPT

functions.name: concernbert.training.select_rand_parent_group
documentation: Return all rows that share a randomly chosen parent_id

documentation: type: ignore

modules.name: concernbert.recovery
documentation: This is so ugly because it was directly converted from a Jupyter Notebook.

functions.name: concernbert.recovery.normalize_vectors
documentation: Normalize each vector in X to have a unit length, handle zero-norm vectors.

variables.name: concernbert.recovery.tree
documentation: type: ignore

functions.name: concernbert.cli.split
documentation:
Split the lines of a text file into train, test, and val sets.

Distributes the lines found in INPUT into newly created files TRAIN, TEST,
and VAL according to the provided percentages. Each line is uniquely
assigned to one of these three files. The order of the lines is preserved.

functions.name: concernbert.cli.extract_files
documentation:
Extract a CSV listing all valid files found inside the dbs.

Reads in each path listed in INPUT and attempts to open it as a SQLite
database. Executes several queries to get file-level data. Writes file-level
rows to OUTPUT.

functions.name: concernbert.cli.extract_entities
documentation:
Extract a parquet table of entities.

Given an INPUT file created by extract-files, this will create a parquet
file at OUTPUT where each row is an entity from one of the files mentioned
in INPUT. Only entities with at least one sibling will be output.

functions.name: concernbert.cli.train
documentation:
Runs the training procedure.

Training arguments are specified in CONFIG_FILE. See config.ini for an
example.

functions.name: concernbert.cli.export_file_ranker
documentation:
Exports a CSV of file pairs that can be used in the fileranker web
application.

functions.name: concernbert.cli.extract_files_from_seq
documentation:
Extract a CSV listing all valid files found inside a sequences.csv.

Like extract-files, but takes a sequences.csv as INPUT. The resulting OUTPUT
csv will contain only the files mentioned in the provided sequences.csv.

functions.name: concernbert.semantic._determine_lineage
documentation:
Calculate the ancestor-descendant relationship between two nodes.

Returns the tuple (ancestor, descendant). If the two nodes do not have
a strict ancestor-descendant relationship, then None is returned.

functions.name: concernbert.semantic._calculate_parents_dict
documentation: Given a list of nodes, return a child-to-parent mapping.
Very slow!

functions.name: concernbert.semantic._to_children_dict
documentation: Return a parent-to-children mapping given a child-to-parent mapping.

methods.name: concernbert.selection.EntityTree.standard_class
documentation:
Returns the id of the standard class if one exists in this file.

A standard class occurs when a file has exactly one root entity. This
root entity is a class with at least two children. All children must be
either attributes or methods.
